summary: Disabled the GitHub Pages publishing workflow by adding `if: false` to the `gen-pages` job in `.github/workflows/publish-pages.yml`. The PR body only says to see #1085 and #1102, so the immediate motivation is linked externally rather than explained in this slice.
what_changed: Added `if: false` to the `gen-pages` job in `.github/workflows/publish-pages.yml`.
Left the workflow file and job definition intact while preventing the `Generate pages` job from running.
No source, headers, matching code, or decompilation symbols were changed.
smart_moves: Used a minimal one-line GitHub Actions condition to disable the job without deleting the workflow, preserving the existing configuration for possible future re-enable.
Placed the disabling condition at the job level, so the entire pages generation/deploy path is skipped rather than relying on individual step conditions.
Referenced related PRs/issues (#1085 and #1102) instead of duplicating context, though the provided slice does not include their details.
decomp_lessons: For repository infrastructure changes, prefer small reversible workflow edits when the intent is to temporarily or administratively disable automation.
Disabling CI/deploy jobs can be done without affecting decompilation code or matching status by using GitHub Actions job-level `if` conditions.
When a workflow is being disabled due to prior incidents or related changes, include enough context in the PR body or linked issues so future maintainers can understand when it is safe to re-enable.
naming_conventions: Workflow file name: `.github/workflows/publish-pages.yml` describes the publishing responsibility.
Job id `gen-pages` and display name `Generate pages` identify the page-generation/deploy job.
follow_up_queries: Look up PR or issue #1085 for the reason the publish-pages workflow needed to be disabled.
Look up PR #1102 for related GitHub Pages or workflow context.
Search later PRs for re-enabling or replacing `.github/workflows/publish-pages.yml`.
